affine.prompt:
Given one integer n on the stack, leave 3*n + 7 on the stack.
Initial stack: n  ->  final stack: 3*n+7.
Examples:  n=0 -> 7   n=1 -> 10   n=5 -> 22   n=2 -> 13

rev3.prompt:
The stack holds three integers a, b, c with c on top. Reverse them.
Initial stack: a b c (c on top)  ->  final stack: c b a (a on top).
Examples:  1 2 3 -> 3 2 1 (bottom..top)    7 8 9 -> 9 8 7

is_even.prompt:
Given one integer n on the stack, leave 1 if n is even else 0.
Initial stack: n  ->  final stack: 1 or 0.
Examples:  0 -> 1   1 -> 0   2 -> 1   3 -> 0   4 -> 1

factorial.prompt:
Given one integer n >= 0 on the stack, leave n! (with 0! = 1).
Initial stack: n  ->  final stack: n!.
Examples:  0 -> 1   1 -> 1   3 -> 6   5 -> 120   6 -> 720

gcd.prompt:
The stack holds two integers a (lower) and b (top). Leave gcd(a, b) (Euclid; gcd(a,0)=a).
Initial stack: a b (b on top)  ->  final stack: gcd(a,b).
Examples:  12 8 -> 4    48 36 -> 12    0 5 -> 5    5 0 -> 5

sum_list.prompt:
The stack holds a list as one quotation of integers. Leave the sum (empty list -> 0).
Initial stack: [x0 x1 ...]  ->  final stack: sum.
Examples:  [1 2 3] -> 6    [5] -> 5    [] -> 0    [10 20 30 40] -> 100

reverse_list.prompt:
The stack holds a list as one quotation of integers. Leave the reversed list as a quotation on the stack.
Initial stack: [x0 x1 ...]  ->  final stack: [... x1 x0].
Examples:  [1 2 3] -> [3 2 1]    [] -> []    [7] -> [7]    [1 2 3 4] -> [4 3 2 1]

palindrome_number.prompt:
Given one integer n >= 0 on the stack, leave 1 if its decimal digits read the same forwards and backwards, else 0.
Initial stack: n  ->  final stack: 1 or 0.
Examples:  121 -> 1   123 -> 0   7 -> 1   1221 -> 1   10 -> 0   0 -> 1

contains.prompt:
The stack holds a list as one quotation (lower) and an integer x on top. Leave 1 if x is in the list else 0.
Initial stack: [x0 x1 ...] x (x on top)  ->  final stack: 1 or 0.
Examples:  [1 2 3] 2 -> 1    [1 2 3] 5 -> 0    [] 5 -> 0    [7] 7 -> 1

climbing_stairs.prompt:
Given one integer n on the stack, leave the number of ways to climb n stairs taking 1 or 2 steps at a time (Fibonacci-like: 0->1, 1->1).
Initial stack: n  ->  final stack: ways.
Examples:  0 -> 1   1 -> 1   2 -> 2   3 -> 3   4 -> 5   5 -> 8
